domain_hint: Conjugate heat transfer simulation of a turbine nozzle guide vane with internal serpentine cooling passages, assessed at PDR stage with several unresolved validation and uncertainty issues
ambiguity_notes: (1) Mesh selection contradiction — executive summary (Slide 12) claims GCI<1% on fine mesh but all production results use medium mesh (GCI 3.2%), and uncertainty budgets do not reflect the correct GCI value. (2) TBC contact resistance discrepancy — Slide 7 uses literature value 2.4e-4 m²·K/W but companion report states in-house measured value of 1.8e-4 m²·K/W; the simulation central value is therefore uncertain by ~25%, shifting T_peak by up to 22 K. (3) Validation scope overstated — Slide 9 and executive summary claim validation against engine hardware, but Dataset B (NGV-3) has significant geometric dissimilarities from NGV-4 (different pass-3 aspect ratio, different film hole count); the claim is technically true but materially misleading. (4) Margin conclusion inconsistency — Slide 9 concludes thermal margins are acceptable, but Slide 8 shows combined uncertainty reduces nominal 37 K margin to ~4 K, making the acceptability conclusion unsupported without qualification.
